headline: B40 Healthcare Spending Rises to 6.8% of Household Income
context: Low-income households spend proportionally more on healthcare despite public system availability due to access barriers.

cards.big: B40 households spend 6.8% of income on healthcare versus 3.2% for T20 in 2025
cards.sub: Out-of-pocket costs for medicines transport and private consultations drive the gap

cards.big: 42% of B40 patients purchase medicines from private pharmacies due to public stock shortages
cards.sub: Drug procurement inefficiencies force patients to seek costly alternatives

cards.big: WHO defines catastrophic health expenditure as exceeding 10% of household income
cards.sub: 8.4% of B40 households already exceed this threshold in Malaysia as of 2025

cards.big: Thailand's Universal Coverage Scheme limits out-of-pocket costs to 30 baht per visit since 2002
cards.sub: Comprehensive public coverage prevents financial hardship from healthcare costs

cards.big: When the poor pay more for worse healthcare the system fails its most fundamental purpose
Should Malaysia cap out-of-pocket health costs for B40 households at 3% of income
